conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 5 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you some questions about your circumstances:

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you look after him and pay for things like his food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for him - I look after him and pay for all his food, clothes, and everything else he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Are you living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex currently in local authority care, or has he been in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any care and hasn't been in hospital.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility criteria before making my final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 5 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him and pay for his needs
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He is not in local authority care or hospital

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex if you haven't already done so.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for him (looking after him and paying for his food, clothes and other needs), nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he is not in local authority care or hospital for more than 12 weeks. All the eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their 5-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex, he lives with them in the UK, he is under 16, no one else is claiming for him, and he is not in care or hospital. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
